task.title: Add comprehensive logging to backend for Docker deployment debugging

context.project: Chatbot-temp — React + Hono + MongoDB chat UI with graph-based backend agent
context.problem: Backend has almost no logging. When someone pulls Docker images and runs the app, they cannot: - See what requests are being made - Know if AGENT_URL is being called or why it fails - Debug why Gemini returns errors - Understand the flow of a chat request end-to-end
context.solution: Add structured logging at key points throughout the backend so operators can debug issues by reading container logs.

requirements.req: Create a reusable logger utility (backend/src/lib/logger.ts) — simple wrapper around console with timestamp, level, and structured JSON-like output. No external dependencies.
Add a request logging middleware in index.ts — logs method, path, status code, and duration for every incoming request (e.g. "[12:34:56] GET /api/conversations/abc → 200 (15ms)")
Add startup log on server listen — print config summary (PORT, AGENT_URL set or not, GEMINI_API_KEY set or not — DO NOT print actual key values, just "[SET]" or "[NOT SET]")

Log in graph-interpreter.ts: - When getGraphData is called: log AGENT_URL being called, request payload summary (query truncated to 80 chars, image present/absent, conversationId, history count) - When agent responds: log status code, response summary (node count, edge count) - On error: log full error message - When Gemini is called: log system instruction truncated (just "building system instruction with N nodes") - When Gemini responds: log response length - When bypass mode (no GEMINI_API_KEY): log "[BYPASS] Returning raw agent response"

Log in chat.ts route: - Request received: conversationId, message length - History loaded: count of messages - interpretGraph() result: response length, title set or not - Error cases: full error logged
Log in upload.ts route: filename, file size, result (success/fail, MinIO URL returned)
Log in conversations.ts: CRUD operation type and result
Log in history.ts: request received, auth result, message count returned
Log level control: use environment variable LOG_LEVEL (default: "info") — supports "debug", "info", "warn", "error". Debug shows payloads, info shows summaries.

constraints.constraint: DO NOT add any npm dependencies — use only Node.js built-ins (console, Date, etc.)
DO NOT log sensitive data: GEMINI_API_KEY values, MINIO_SECRET_KEY, user message content in full (truncate to 80 chars)
Log format: [HH:MM:SS] [LEVEL] message — color-coded for terminal readability if possible
Each modified file should stay under 200 lines

log-format-examples.example: [12:34:58] [DEBUG] Agent calling POST http://agent:8080 — query="tìm Nguyễn Văn A" (33 chars), history=2, image=none
[12:34:59] [WARN] [BYPASS] No GEMINI_API_KEY — returning raw agent JSON

verification.step: Run: cd backend && npm run build — must compile without errors
Run: cd backend && npm run dev — check logs appear on startup and on each request
Verify: docker-compose logs -f backend shows readable, timestamped log entries
Verify: no API keys or secrets appear in logs
